messages.content: Say a new resepi for cake

Banana and Chocolate Chip Cake

Ingredients:
- 2 ripe bananas
- 1/2 cup unsalted butter, softened
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 2 large eggs
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1 cup chocolate chips

Directions:

1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 9-inch cake pan.
2. In a large bowl, mash the bananas until smooth.
3. In a separate bowl, cream the butter and sugar together until light and fluffy.
4. Add the eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
5. Stir in the vanilla extract and mashed bananas.
6. In another bowl, sift the flour, baking soda, and salt together. Gradually add to the banana mixture, mixing until just combined.
7. Fold in the chocolate chips.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
9. Bake for 45-50 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
10. Let the cake cool in the pan for 10 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.

Enjoy your delicious Banana and Chocolate Chip Cake!
A new cake whit creative approach

Caramel Apple Upside-Down Cake

Ingredients:
- 3 medium-sized apples, peeled and sliced
- 1/2 cup unsalted butter, room temperature
- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 1 tsp cinnamon
- 1/4 tsp nutmeg
- 1/2 cup granulated sugar
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 2 large eggs
- 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
- 2 tsp baking powder
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1/2 cup milk
- Caramel sauce (homemade or store-bought)

Directions:

1. Preheat the oven to 350°F and prepare a 9-inch cake pan by greasing it. 
2. In a small bowl, mix the brown sugar, cinnamon, and nutmeg together. Pour the mixture into the greased pan and spread it evenly on the bottom. 
3. Arrange the sliced apples on top of the sugar mixture.
4. In a large bowl, cream the butter and granulated sugar together until light and fluffy.
5. Beat in the vanilla extract and eggs, one at a time.
6. In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt. Gradually stir in the dry ingredients into the butter mixture.
7. Pour the milk over the batter and blend until fully incorporated.
8. Pour the batter over the apples in the pan, making sure it's even.
9. Bake the cake for 35-40 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center of the cake comes out clean.
10. Let the cake cool for 10 minutes before inverting it onto a serving dish.
11. Drizzle caramel sauce evenly over the top of the cake.
12. Serve slightly warm or at room temperature for a delicious and unique twist on traditional upside-down cakes.

Enjoy your incredibly delicious Caramel Apple Upside-Down Cake!
A creative and new cake that not cooked everyone

No-Bake Mango Cheesecake

Ingredients:
- 2 cups graham cracker crumbs
- 1/2 cup unsalted butter, melted
- 4 packages (32 oz) cream cheese, softened
- 1 1/4 cups granulated sugar
- 1/4 cup lemon juice
- 2 tsp vanilla extract
- 2 cups heavy cream
- 3-4 ripe mangos, peeled and pureed
- Mango chunks and whipped cream for garnish

Directions:

1. In a bowl, mix together the graham cracker crumbs and melted butter.
2. Press the crumbs into the bottom and up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan. Chill in the refrigerator for 15-20 minutes.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat the cream cheese until smooth. 
4. Add sugar, lemon juice, and vanilla extract and continue to beat until the mixture is creamy and smooth.
5. Gradually beat in the heavy cream until the mixture thickens.
6. Add the pureed mango to the mixture and beat until fully combined.
7. Pour the mixture over the chilled crust.
8. Chill the cheesecake for at least 6 hours or until set.
9. Decorate the top with mango chunks and whipped cream.
10. Slice and serve chilled.

Enjoy your sweet and tangy no-bake mango cheesecake!
A creative and new food that not cooked everyone

Rainbow Veggie Wraps with Hummus

Ingredients:
- 6-8 large collard green leaves
- 1 red bell pepper, sliced
- 1 yellow bell pepper, sliced
- 1 green bell pepper, sliced
- 2 medium carrots, julienned
- 1 cucumber, sliced
- 1/2 cup hummus
- 1/4 cup chopped fresh cilantro
- 1/4 cup chopped fresh mint
- Salt and pepper to taste

Directions:

1. Rinse the collard green leaves and gently pat dry.
2. Cut off the spine of the collard green leaves so it's flat.
3. Heat a skillet over medium to high heat and grill the bell peppers until slightly charred. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
4. To assemble the wrap, spread hummus onto the center of each collard green leaf. 
5. Arrange the grilled bell peppers, carrots, and cucumber slices on top of the hummus.
6. Sprinkle fresh cilantro and mint on top.
7. Fold one end of the collard green leaf over the veggies and tuck under, then start rolling up the collard green leaf tightly.
8. Slice in half diagonally and serve immediately.

Enjoy your healthy, colorful, and flavorful Rainbow Veggie Wraps with Hummus!
Tell me another food

Oven Roasted Lemon Garlic Chicken Thighs

Ingredients:
- 6-8 chicken thighs, bone-in and skin-on
- 2 lemons, cut into wedges
- 8 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 1 tsp dried oregano
- 1 tsp dried thyme
- Salt and pepper to taste

Directions:

1. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
2. In a bowl, mix together the minced garlic, olive oil, dried oregano, and dried thyme.
3. Pat the chicken thighs dry and season generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
4. Spread the garlic and herb mixture onto the chicken thighs, making sure it's fully coated.
5. Place the chicken thighs and lemon wedges onto a baking pan.
6. Bake in the preheated oven for 35-40 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through and has an internal temperature of 165°F.
7. Squeeze the roasted lemon wedges over the chicken before serving.

Enjoy your juicy and flavorful Oven Roasted Lemon Garlic Chicken Thighs!
Tell me another that new and nobody don't cooked

Grilled Peach and Burrata Salad

Ingredients:
- 2 ripe peaches, pitted and cut into wedges
- 2 cups arugula
- 1/2 cup fresh basil leaves
- 1 ball of burrata cheese
- 3 tbsp olive oil, divided
- 2 tbsp balsamic vinegar
- 1 tbsp honey
- Salt and pepper to taste

Directions:

1. Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
2. Toss the peach wedges in 1 tbsp olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
3. Grill the peaches for 2-3 minutes on each side until slightly charred and softened.
4. In a small bowl, whisk together 2 tbsp olive oil, balsamic vinegar, and honey until well combined.
5. In a large bowl, mix together the arugula and fresh basil leaves.
6. Drizzle the balsamic dressing over the greens and toss until fully coated.
7. Arrange the grilled peach wedges on top of the greens.
8. Tear the burrata cheese and evenly distribute it over the salad.
9. Season with additional salt and pepper to taste.

Enjoy your delicious and unique Grilled Peach and Burrata Salad!
Tell me another that new and nobody don't cooked

Roasted Beet and Goat Cheese Tartlets

Ingredients:
- 2 medium-sized beets, peeled and diced
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 1 sheet puff pastry, thawed
- 4 oz goat cheese, softened
- 2 tbsp Greek yogurt
- 1 tsp fresh thyme leaves
- 1 egg, beaten

Directions:

1. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. In a bowl, toss the diced beets in olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
3. Spread the beets onto a baking sheet and roast in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes until tender and slightly caramelized.
4. While the beets are roasting, roll out the puff pastry sheet on a lightly floured surface. Cut the puff pastry into 4 squares and transfer each square onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
5. In a small bowl, mix together the goat cheese, Greek yogurt, and fresh thyme leaves.
6. Evenly distribute the goat cheese mixture onto the center of each puff pastry square.
7. Once the beets are done roasting, spoon the roasted beets on top of the goat cheese mixture.
8. Fold the edges of the puff pastry up to create a rustic tartlet shape.
9. Brush the edges of the puff pastry with beaten egg.
10. Bake the tartlets in the oven for 20-25 minutes or until the puff pastry is golden brown and fully cooked.

Enjoy your unique and flavorful Roasted Beet and Goat Cheese Tartlets!

Crispy Tofu with Orange Glaze

Ingredients:
- 1 block of firm tofu, pressed and sliced into triangles
- 1/2 cup cornstarch
- 1/4 cup vegetable oil
- 1/2 cup orange juice
- 1 tbsp soy sauce
- 2 tbsp honey
- 1 tbsp sesame oil
- 1 tbsp rice vinegar
- 1 tbsp grated ginger
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- Sesame seeds and green onions for garnish

Directions:

1. Spread the cornstarch out in a shallow bowl. Dredge the tofu triangles in the cornstarch, shaking off excess.
2. Heat the vegetable oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Add the tofu to the pan and fry for about 2-3 minutes on each side until crispy and golden brown. Remove and place on a paper towel to drain excess oil.
3. In a saucepan over medium heat, whisk together the orange juice, soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, rice vinegar, ginger, and garlic.
4. Let the sauce simmer for about 5-7 minutes until it thickens and becomes syrupy.
5. Drizzle the orange glaze over the crispy tofu triangles.
6. Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions.

Enjoy your crispy, sweet, and tangy Crispy Tofu with Orange Glaze!
Tell me another that new and nobody don’t cooked that delicious for Iranian tasted

Saffron Chicken Kebabs

Ingredients:
- 1 lb boneless chicken breasts, cut into small cubes
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 1/4 tsp ground black pepper
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 2 tsp ground saffron dissolved in 2 tbsp hot water
- 2 tbsp honey
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- 1 onion, cut into thin slices
- 1 red bell pepper, cut into small pieces
- 1 green bell pepper, cut into small pieces
- 6-8 skewers, soaked in water for at least an hour

Directions:

1. In a bowl, mix together the olive oil, black pepper, salt, dissolved saffron, honey, and lemon juice.
2. Add the chicken cubes into the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes (or up to 24 hours) to marinate.
3. Preheat a grill or grill pan to high heat.
4. Thread the marinated chicken, onion, and bell peppers onto the skewers in an alternating pattern.
5. Grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally until the chicken is cooked through and has an internal temperature of 165°F.
6. Serve the Saffron Chicken Kebabs with basmati rice or flatbread.

Enjoy your delicious and aromatic Saffron Chicken Kebabs, perfect for Iranian palate!

Spinach and Feta Stuffed Persian Meatballs (Koofteh)

Ingredients:
For the Meatballs:
- 1 lb ground beef or lamb
- 1/2 cup cooked basmati rice
- 1/2 onion, grated
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped
- 1 egg, beaten
- 1 tsp ground turmeric
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Oil for frying

For the Filling:
- 2 cups fresh spinach, chopped
- 1/2 cup crumbled feta cheese
- 1/2 onion, chopped
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- Salt and pepper to taste

For the Sauce:
- 2 cups beef or chicken broth
- 1/2 onion, chopped
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp tomato paste
- 1 tsp ground turmeric
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- Salt and pepper to taste

Directions:

1. In a small pan, heat the olive oil and sauté the chopped onions until softened. Add the chopped spinach and cook until wilted, then remove from heat and let cool.
2. Mix together the ground beef or lamb, cooked basmati rice, grated onion, minced garlic, chopped parsley, beaten egg, ground turmeric, salt, and pepper in a large mixing bowl until well combined.
3. Shape the meat mixture into small balls, then flatten them out into small patties. Spoon the spinach and feta filling into the centers of the patties and seal the edges, forming meatballs.
4. Heat oil in a large skillet and fry the meatballs until they are browned on all sides and cooked until no longer pink in the center.
5. Remove the meatballs from the skillet and set aside on a plate.
6. In the same skillet, sauté the chopped onions and garlic until softened. Add the tomato paste, ground turmeric, ground cumin, salt, and pepper to the skillet and stir well.
7. Pour the chicken broth into the skillet and stir the mixture.
8. Return the meatballs to the skillet and bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20-25 minutes until the sauce has thickened.
9. Serve the Spinach and Feta Stuffed Persian Meatballs hot with the sauce.

Enjoy your delicious and flavorful Spinach and Feta Stuffed Persian Meatballs (Koofteh)!
Tell me another that new and nobody don’t cooked that delicious for Iranian tasted

Zereshk Polo ba Morgh (Saffron Rice with Barberry and Chicken)

Ingredients:
- 2 cups basmati rice, rinsed and soaked for at least an hour
- 2 tbsp butter
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
- 1/2 tsp ground cumin
- 1/2 tsp ground cardamom
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 1/4 tsp saffron threads, dissolved in 2 tbsp hot water
- 1 cup barberries, washed and dried
- 2 tbsp sugar
- 1/4 cup water
- 6 bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- Salt and pepper to taste

Directions:

1. In a large pot, bring 8-10 cups of water to a boil. Add the soaked basmati rice to the pot and cook for 6-8 minutes until partially cooked. Drain the rice and set it aside.
2. In a large saucepan, melt the butter over medium-high heat. Add the chopped onion and spices (ground cinnamon, ground cumin, ground cardamom, salt, and pepper). Sauté until the onion is softened and fragrant.
3. Add the partially cooked basmati rice to the saucepan and toss to coat well with the onion and spice mixture.
4. Pour the dissolved saffron over the rice, cover the pot, and reduce heat to low. Cook the rice for 15-20 minutes until fully cooked.
5. In a separate saucepan, combine the barberries, sugar, and water. Heat the mixture over medium heat until it comes to a simmer. Simmer for 5-7 minutes until the barberries have softened and the sugar has dissolved.
6. Season the chicken thighs with salt and pepper. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken thighs to the skillet and cook for 6-8 minutes on each side until browned and crispy.
7. Serve the saffron rice topped with the cooked barberries and chicken thighs.

Enjoy your fragrant and delicious Zereshk Polo ba Morgh (Saffron Rice with Barberry and Chicken)!

Kashk-e Bademjan (Eggplant and Whey Dip)

Ingredients:
- 2 large eggplants, roasted and mashed
- 1/2 cup whey (or substitute with Greek yogurt)
- 1/4 cup chopped walnuts
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 tbsp dried mint
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 4 tbsp olive oil

Directions:

1. Preheat the oven to 400°F. Cut the eggplants in half lengthwise and place them cut side up on a baking sheet. Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with salt and pepper. Roast the eggplants in the preheated oven for 30-40 minutes until they are tender and fully cooked.
2. Once the eggplants are roasted, remove from the oven and let cool. Once cooled, mash the flesh with a fork until it is mostly smooth without many large chunks.
3. In a small pan over low heat, warm the whey (or Greek yogurt) until it is barely warm to the touch. Do not let it boil or it will curdle.
4. Add the warmed whey, minced garlic,
